hypothesis: If the semantic alignment between a scientific task and the available tools is increased (via higher task–tool embedding similarity, more workflow-specific tool descriptions, task decomposition, or a hypothesis generator), then LLM agent performance (e.g., pass@k/success rate) will improve more than it does from switching agent scaffolds (ReAct vs tool-calling) or LLMs (Claude-3.5 vs GPT-4o), because alignment reduces tool-selection and planning errors that dominate outcomes.
